properties.name: Kraken of the Norwegian Sea
properties.summary: A vast many-armed terror said to drag entire ships beneath the waves off the Norwegian coast.

properties.name: Sea Serpent of Gloucester
properties.summary: A long, humped serpent reported by sailors off the New England coast, gliding just beneath the surface before vanishing.

properties.name: Serpent of Loch Ness
properties.summary: A shy lake-dwelling serpent, glimpsed only in passing by those who happen to look at exactly the right moment.

properties.name: Leviathan of the Deep Atlantic
properties.summary: The biblical sea monster created on the fifth day, so vast that the ocean is merely its bath. When it stirs, the tides of the world reverse.

properties.name: Jörmungandr — Midgard Serpent
properties.summary: The World Serpent, child of Loki, coiled around the entire ocean floor with its tail in its own mouth. When it releases its bite, Ragnarok begins.

properties.name: Makara of the Indian Ocean
properties.summary: The great sea-beast of Hindu myth — part crocodile, part fish, part elephant — on whose back the god Varuna rides across the deep.

properties.name: Kraken of the Pacific Abyss
properties.summary: Sailors crossing the vast Pacific speak of dark shapes rising beneath their hulls — a many-armed shadow large enough to blot out the moon's reflection.

properties.name: Ryūjin's Serpent Guard
properties.summary: The great sea dragon that circles the palace of Ryūjin in the depths of the Pacific, controlling the tides with the magical jewels it holds in its coils.

properties.name: Tianlong — Celestial Dragon of the South Sea
properties.summary: One of the four dragon kings of Chinese myth, master of the South Sea, who brings rain when pleased and drowns fleets when angered.

properties.name: Kraken of the Southern Pacific
properties.summary: Polynesian navigators warned of a vast creature sleeping in the deep south waters whose breath causes sudden whirlpools and whose dreams bring storms.

properties.name: Cipactli — Aztec Sea Monster
properties.summary: The primordial crocodilian beast that floated on the primal sea before creation. The gods Tezcatlipoca and Quetzalcoatl tore it apart to make the earth — but it still hungers.

properties.name: Imap Ukua — Inuit Sea Serpent
properties.summary: A colossal serpent of the Arctic waters, known to the Inuit as the master of sea creatures. Shamans must appease it or the seals and whales will vanish from the ice.

properties.name: Kraken of the South Atlantic
properties.summary: Recorded in the logs of Portuguese and Dutch sailors rounding the Cape — a vast darkness rising from below that capsized two ships and left no survivors to describe its face.
